response.justification: Bogdan has genuine Staff-level pedigree and credible high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s, low-latency async I/O), and his current role at re:cinq places him directly in an agentic engineering context building MCP tooling and AI-agent frameworks. However, Python — a stated must-have — is entirely absent from his skills and work history, which is a material gap. The AI/agentic work is also very recent and described at a surface level with no measurable outcomes, making it hard to assess depth. Operational ownership, on-call, and agile experience are not mentioned anywhere in the resume. He is worth a screen call to probe the Python gap and depth of agentic work, but should not advance without satisfactory answers.
strengths: Hands-on high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async microservices
Current role directly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, showing active engagement with agentic engineering workflows
Consistent Staff-level titling and demonstrated technical breadth across embedded, web, mobile, and infra domains over a 15+ year career
concerns: Python is completely absent from both the skills list and all work history — this is a stated must-have for the role
The AI and agentic work at re:cinq began in April 2025 and is described in only two brief sentences with no depth, scope, or measurable impact
No mention of operational ownership, on-call rotations, monitoring, incident response, or agile/scrum practices anywhere in the resume
